steps.title: Compare the two scopes
steps.content: Fodor's hypothesis treats low-level systems as modular but not high-level systems. Carruthers's massive modularity hypothesis expands modularity to all cognitive systems. The key comparison is therefore about how broad each definition of modularity is.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate contrasts
steps.content: Choice A does not clearly compare the hypotheses. Choice B falsely says Fodor denied innate neural structures. Choice D falsely says they differ over low-level systems, which both treat as modular. The choice about Fodorian modularity being less expansive captures the real scope difference. The answer is C.
